title: catevala subfasciata
intro: Catevala subfasciata, also known as the Mexican buckeye or Catclaw, is a small tree or shrub native to Mexico, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Catevala subfasciata include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, have been used to treat various ailments, and its seeds are also used as a natural insecticide.
